description: The creative capacity to effect change, understood as a force that flows through a community and is strongest when shared. It is defined by 'power with' and 'power to', not 'power over'.
rationale: The word 'kio' reframes the concept of power. The initial syllable 'ki-' uses a clean plosive to signify capacity and the insightful 'i' to suggest skillful means (Buddhist). The vowel hiatus 'i-o' sonically represents the core concept: individual capacity ('i') flowing into and contributing to the collective wholeness ('o'), reflecting Solarpunk and Peace Linguistics ideals of shared, non-hoarded power.
